title: Frameworks registry + OER source registry (licence-classified)
context: OER are abundant and free but are not indexed against the curriculum standards that govern what teachers must teach, so teachers cannot find the free resource aligned to a given standard. This project builds an open crosswalk of educator-reviewed AlignmentRecords linking OER to standards by stable identifier (CASE GUID / ASN URI), not by reproducing copyrighted standards text. Two hard gates never skip: the dual licence/terms gate (gate-002) and educator review of every record, with a blind second-reviewer precision audit holding precision at >= 95%. The registries record which frameworks and OER sources are licence/terms-eligible and where their machine-readable data lives.
objective: Author the licence-classified frameworks registry and OER source registry.
acceptanceCriteria: Frameworks registry lists each in-scope framework with its terms classification (permitsReference, permitsTextReproduction) and machine-readable source (CASE registry / official open data).
OER source registry lists candidate OER sources with licence id/url, permitsDerivatives and commercialUse, classified per gate-002 (and ncpolicy-003 for NC).
Each entry cites the licence/terms URL and records a snapshot reference; unparseable/unknown entries flagged EXCLUDE.
Output licensed CC-BY-4.0; the commit is DCO signed-off.
